ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; no specific code exists for a boxed combo of deep-fried chicken wings with curry puffs. Closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM covers deep-fried chicken wings as the dominant component. The curry puff component is not separately coded; composite dish approximation applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Singaporean fried chicken wing and curry puff combo product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately half a box (500 g), comprising roughly 5 deep-fried chicken wings and 1 curry puff, though the full box of 10 wings and 2 curry puffs (approximately 1000 g) is commonly shared between 2–3 people.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, frozen, glazed, reheated (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); supplemented with HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition of Food for fried chicken and curry puff components
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact combo product (10 deep-fried chicken wings + 2 Old Chang Kee Curry'O curry puffs). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for deep-fried chicken wings (NDB No. 05062, chicken wing with skin, fried, batter-coated) and HPB Singapore data for fried chicken and curry puff. The input per_100g values (energy 320 kcal, protein 18 g, fat 24 g, carbohydrate 10 g) are consistent with USDA fried batter-coated chicken wing data and are retained with medium confidence. Composite dish weighting applied: approximately 80% chicken wing, 20% curry puff by mass.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fried battered chicken and wheat-flour pastry
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific combo product. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on analogous entries: deep-fried battered chicken (GI ~60–65, wheat flour coating dominant carbohydrate source) and curry puff pastry (GI ~65–70, refined wheat flour pastry). Weighted composite GI of approximately 65 applied. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 50) / 100 = 32.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 450 mg/100 g; saturated fat 8.0 g/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 450 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension concern, and a full box (1000 g) delivers approximately 4500 mg sodium — nearly double the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg — making this product unsuitable for individuals with hypertension; portion should be strictly limited to 2–3 wings maximum per sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate from wheat flour coating and curry puff pastry; high fat content worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: The wheat flour batter coating and curry puff pastry contribute rapidly digestible refined carbohydrates (GI ~65, GL ~32.5 per 500 g serving), while the high fat content (24 g/100 g) from deep-frying promotes insulin resistance; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product or limit intake to 2–3 wings without the curry puff and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.0 g/100 g; trans fat 0.15 g/100 g; cholesterol 120 mg/100 g; sodium 450 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat at 8.0 g/100 g, trans fat at 0.15 g/100 g, and cholesterol at 120 mg/100 g from deep-frying in oil with chicken skin collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk; combined with high sodium (450 mg/100 g) contributing to arterial stiffness, this product is unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) from deep-frying; saturated fat 8.0 g/100 g exceeding 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that directly exacerbate joint inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis, and saturated fat at 8.0 g/100 g far exceeds the 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried preparations entirely and opt for grilled or baked chicken alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Wing Box of 10 with Curry Puffs is a popular Singaporean fried snack combo product sold on GrabFood Singapore, associated with the Old Chang Kee brand. The box contains ten deep-fried chicken wings coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter, accompanied by two complimentary Old Chang Kee Curry'O curry puffs filled with spiced potato and onion curry enclosed in a flaky wheat flour pastry shell. The chicken wings are deep-fried in hot oil until the skin is golden-brown and crispy, yielding a juicy interior with a crunchy, well-seasoned exterior. The curry puffs contribute a mild, aromatic curry flavour with a buttery pastry texture. Per 100 g, this combo delivers approximately 320 kcal, 18 g protein, 24 g fat (of which 8 g is saturated), 10 g carbohydrate, 0.8 g sugar, 1.0 g dietary fibre, and 450 mg sodium. The high sodium content (450 mg/100 g), elevated saturated fat (8.0 g/100 g), trans fat (0.15 g/100 g), and cholesterol (120 mg/100 g) make this product clinically unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The product is halal-certified and contains gluten from the wheat flour coating and pastry. It is best consumed as an occasional snack and is commonly shared among 2–3 people. The dish reflects Singapore's vibrant hawker and fast-food snack culture, combining Western-style fried chicken with the iconic local curry puff.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact combo product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central deep-fried battered chicken wing data and HPB Singapore fried chicken and curry puff data. The input values are consistent with these approved sources and are retained with medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried battered chicken and wheat flour pastry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this specific composite Singaporean fried chicken wing and curry puff combo product are not available in USDA FoodData Central or the Singapore HPB Nutrient Composition database as of 2026. The input source listed 'https://foodo.sg/' under the HPB organization but this URL does not correspond to the official HPB nutrient database; the approved HPB source (https://www.hpb.gov.sg/) and USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/) were used instead. The portion entry 'One box containing 12 Curry'Os' with 12.0 g was flagged as implausible and discarded; the primary portion of 1000 g for the full box is retained. The original input listed two portions both marked is_default: true, which is inconsistent; only the 1000 g full-box portion is treated as default. Halal status is retained from input cultural.dietary_tags. All core macros (protein_g 18.0, fat_g 24.0, carbohydrate_g 10.0) are non-zero and consistent with USDA fried battered chicken wing data; no zero-macro justification is required. English entries: 'Chicken Wing Box of 10 (Free Curry'O x 2)', 'Fried Chicken Wing Box with Curry Puffs', 'OCK Chicken Wing Box', 'Old Chang Kee Chicken Wing Box', 'Chicken Wings with Curry Puffs', 'Deep-fried chicken wings combo box Singapore' — all present. Chinese entries: '炸鸡翅盒装（附咖喱角）', '炸雞翅盒裝（附咖喱角）' — all present. Malay entries: 'Kotak Sayap Ayam Goreng dengan Karipap', 'Sayap Ayam Goreng Kotak' — all present. Tamil entries: 'வறுத்த கோழி இறக்கை பெட்டி (கறி பஃப் உடன்)', 'தொகுப்பு கோழி இறக்கை' — all present. Hindi entries: 'तले हुए चिकन विंग्स बॉक्स (करी पफ के साथ)', 'चिकन विंग्स बॉक्स' — all present.
